extra.reasoning:
The property G(opted_out -> not sent) is VIOLATED within 10 events (bound is 11).

Two transitions set sent:=true: t2 (send_attempt when NOT opted_out, safe) and t28 (send_attempt when opted_out AND stage>=8, the hazard). The property is violated if stage can reach 8 while opted_out is true.

Critical interaction flaws: (1) opted_out is set true by t1 and is NEVER reset to false by any transition, so it latches. (2) The stage-advancing transitions t3-t26 do NOT guard on opted_out, so the stage can still be navigated to 8 after opting out. (3) t28 then re-enables sending at stage>=8 even though opted_out is true.

The stage-advance combination to reach stage 8 from 0 is: key_1 (t3: 0->1), key_2 (t6: 1->2), key_0 (t9: 2->3), key_2 (t12: 3->4), key_0 (t15: 4->5), key_1 (t18: 5->6), key_0 (t21: 6->7), key_0 (t24: 7->8).

Trace of the witness:
1. optout (t1): opted_out=true, sent=false, stage=0
2. key_1 (t3): stage=1
3. key_2 (t6): stage=2
4. key_0 (t9): stage=3
5. key_2 (t12): stage=4
6. key_0 (t15): stage=5
7. key_1 (t18): stage=6
8. key_0 (t21): stage=7
9. key_0 (t24): stage=8
10. send_attempt: opted_out=true, so t2 disabled; t27 (stage<8) disabled; t28 (opted_out and stage>=8) FIRES -> sent=true.

Final state: opted_out=true AND sent=true, directly contradicting opted_out -> not sent. Hence the property is violated.
